channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Seven women die as roof collapses at BISP payment shop in Rahim Yar Khan
item.description: In a tragic incident in Rahim Yar Khan’s Chak 123-P Tibba area, at least seven women lost their lives and many others sustained injuries when the roof of a shop collapsed on Monday morning. The shop w...

item.title: Court Reschedules Raphael Tuju’s Case Against East African Development Bank
item.description: Former Cabinet Secretary Raphael Tuju’s legal battle with the East African Development Bank (EADB) hit an unexpected snag on Monday, March 16, 2026. Tuju’s team was prepared for a 9 a.m virtual hearin...

item.title: Three Fishermen from NSW Die in Boating Accident off South Australia’s Limestone Coast
item.description: Three men from Coleambally in New South Wales tragically lost their lives during a fishing trip on South Australia’s Limestone Coast. Les DeMamiel, 74, Russell Fisher, 67, and John McKenzie, 65, went ...

item.title: Indomie launches AI-powered platform to create personalised praise songs for mothers
item.description: For dis year Mother’s Day, Indomie don use technology blend with culture for something wey no too dey common. Dem don launch platform wey go allow people upload picture of their mama, put her name, ch...

item.title: Provincial bus fare hike forces low-income commuters to make tough choices amid rising fuel costs
item.description: A recent increase in provincial bus fares, which took effect on March 14, 2026, has placed a heavy burden on low-income Filipinos, especially those traveling between Metro Manila and their home provin...

item.title: Iran Allows Some Countries’ Ships Safe Passage Through Strait of Hormuz Amid Conflict
item.description: Iran has declared the Strait of Hormuz open for shipping to all countries except the United States and its allies amid the ongoing US-Israeli conflict with Tehran. The strait, a crucial route for abou...

item.title: PMD Forecasts Rain and Thunderstorms Across Karachi and Sindh on March 18-19
item.description: KARACHI: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has issued a forecast indicating that Karachi and several districts of Sindh will experience rain and thunderstorms on the 18th and 19th of March....

item.title: Labor introduces stricter rules for migration agents to prevent misconduct
item.description: The Albanese government is implementing tighter regulations for migration agents to improve the integrity of Australia's visa system. Starting in April, registered agents will be required to undertake...

item.title: Government considers 1% vote threshold for municipal council representation
item.description: The South African government is considering amending the Local Government: Municipal Structures Act of 1998 to introduce a 1% electoral threshold for political parties seeking representation in munici...

item.title: Ryan Coogler's Sinners secures four Oscars, marking a milestone for Black-led cinema
item.description: Ryan Coogler's film Sinners achieved notable success at the 2026 Oscars, earning four awards from a record 16 nominations. The wins included Best Actor for Michael B. Jordan in his technically challen...

item.title: Iran Maintains Strong Oil Exports Through the Strait of Hormuz Despite Regional Conflict
item.description: Despite a growing Middle East conflict and significant U.S. military attacks, Iran continues to export nearly one million barrels of oil per day through the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global shipping r...

item.title: Roof Collapse at Rahim Yar Khan Shop Claims Six Lives, Several Injured
item.description: On Monday in Rahim Yar Khan’s Chak 123-P Tibba area, a tragic accident occurred when the roof of a local shop collapsed, killing at least six women and injuring numerous others. Some of the deceased w...

item.title: Former SAPS marksman arrested for murder of Witness D
item.description: Police have apprehended Matipandile Sotheni, a former elite SAPS officer, who is accused of murdering Marius van der Merwe, known as Witness D at the Madlanga Commission. Authorities describe Sotheni ...

item.title: Widow sues energy company over husband's electrocution death while pruning hedge in Cheshire
item.description: Blair Campbell, a 35-year-old landscape gardener and father of two, tragically died in October 2022 after being electrocuted while pruning an ivy-covered hedge at a property in Mobberley, near Wilmslo...

item.title: GOP Divided on Trump’s 2020 Election Focus Ahead of Midterms
item.description: As the 2026 midterm elections approach, a growing number of Republican strategists and state party leaders are urging former President Donald Trump to shift his focus away from revisiting the 2020 ele...

item.title: How to Set SBS News as Your Preferred Source on Google in Australia
item.description: Google has rolled out a feature in Australia allowing users to select preferred news sources, giving greater visibility to chosen outlets in Top Stories. SBS News, recognised as one of Australia's mos...

item.title: Getrude Lamu’s Leadership Journey and Promotion of Women in TEAGTL
item.description: Getrude Lamu, Head of Administration at Tanzania East Africa Gateway Terminal Limited (TEAGTL), is a clear example of how women leadership can inspire growth not just for oneself but also for others. ...

item.title: Justice Lenaola calls for ethical legal training and inclusion of former judges in law schools
item.description: Supreme Court Judge Isaac Lenaola has emphasised the need for strong ethical standards in Kenya's legal profession. Speaking at the launch of Zetech University's Law School and LL.B programme at the M...

item.title: Why Global Climate Finance Systems Dey Fail Africa and Wetin We Fit Do to Amend Am
item.description: Climate change no be future thing for Africa again; e don turn current wahala. Floods dey destroy infrastructure wey take years to build, drought dey spoil harvests and scatter people, extreme heat de...

item.title: Two men killed in separate ambushes in Cotabato’s BARMM towns
item.description: Two separate ambush attacks in neighboring towns under the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM), located in Cotabato province, resulted in the deaths of two Moro men and left anothe...
